# Live LSP integration tests.
#
# Requires: gopls on PATH (go install golang.org/x/tools/gopls@latest  OR  brew install gopls)
# This test creates a minimal Go project in $WORK and exercises the LSP client.

env HOME=$WORK/home
mkdir $WORK/home

# Initialize a minimal Go module
exec go mod init testmod

# Create a minimal Go file
exec sh -c 'printf "package main\n\nfunc hello() string {\n\treturn \"hello\"\n}\n\nfunc main() {\n\tprintln(hello())\n}\n" > main.go'

# Test 1: server detection (does not need gopls to be spawned)
sin-code lsp servers
stdout 'go'
stdout 'binary=gopls'

# Test 2: document symbols
sin-code lsp symbols main.go
stdout 'hello'
stdout 'main'

# Test 3: hover on hello() at line 2 (0-indexed), col 5
sin-code lsp hover main.go 2 5
! stdout '(no hover info)'

# Test 4: definition of hello() call at line 7 (0-indexed), col 12
sin-code lsp definition main.go 7 12
! stdout '(no results)'

# Test 5: references to hello() at line 2 (0-indexed), col 5
sin-code lsp references main.go 2 5
! stdout '(no results)'

# Test 6: format on a deliberately bad file
exec sh -c 'printf "package main\nfunc main(){println(\"hi\")}\n" > bad.go'
sin-code lsp format bad.go
! stdout '(no edits needed)'
